creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
I thought I knew it all, but I was being a fool for such a long time 🫣  It’s confession time today, and this one is so stupid. I’ve probably been to this city +30 and even lived in the country, but I guess in my mind, I was still the 14-year-old brat who did NOT want to be in that place at all 🤠

What are the worst travel mistakes you’ve ever made?

transcription.text: I made a massive mistake as a tourist in London. I thought I had it all figured out after working as a London tour guide and running a travel agency that specialised in the UK, but I was a fool. Nowadays it's easy to hop on social media and look for hidden gems and local favourite things to do when we travel, but sometimes, just sometimes, the most famous attractions are famous for a reason. So after spending 15 years trying to explore every nook and cranny of London, I returned to the British Museum and realised how foolish I've been all these years for not coming back and seeing this place with my adult eyes. I hold my hands up, the British Museum is a top thing to do in London that shouldn't be ignored just because it's one of those super touristy attractions.
